skill.name: faq-generator
skill.description: Generates FAQ lists from complex medical policies or protocols. Trigger when user provides medical documents, policies, or protocols and requests FAQ generation, patient education materials, or simplified explanations.
skill.summary: Generates structured FAQ lists from medical policies and protocols, with audience-specific language for patients, researchers, or general readers.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 52 issues across 4 files (538 lines) with a risk score of 100/100. After manual evaluation, the vast majority are false positives: 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections matched non-crypto documentation text and audit JSON metadata; 'Ruby/shell backtick' detections were triggered by Markdown inline code formatting; 'system reconnaissance' hits matched documentation about scope boundaries; and the 'path traversal' detection matched a security checklist item documenting that path traversal should be prevented. The only legitimate concern is that the skill instructs execution of its packaged Python script (scripts/main.py), which is a simple stdlib-only script with hardcoded FAQ templates, no network access, no file I/O, and no environment variable access. One hardcoded GitHub URL exists in documentation for source attribution. Overall risk is low and the skill is safe to publish.

medium_findings.title: External Python Script Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs shell execution of packaged Python scripts ('python scripts/main.py', 'python -m py_compile scripts/main.py'). The script uses only standard library imports with hardcoded data and no network or filesystem access. Risk is residual and limited to script modification or replacement scenarios.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in Documentation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains a hardcoded GitHub URL pointing to the source repository. The URL serves as attribution only and no network requests are made by the actual Python code.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Text in Security Checklist
low_findings.description: SKILL.md security checklist contains the text '../' as part of an item documenting that unauthorized filesystem access should be prevented. This is a documentation artifact, not actual path traversal code.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (19 instances)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer incorrectly flagged non-crypto content in SKILL.md documentation text and faq-generator_audit_result_v1.json audit metadata as weak cryptographic algorithms. No cryptographic operations exist in any file.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution (30 instances)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged Markdown inline code backticks used for formatting file paths and command names as shell backtick execution. These are standard Markdown code spans, not executable backtick commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance (4 instances)
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged documentation text describing skill scope boundaries and output discipline as system reconnaissance. These are legitimate documentation statements, not reconnaissance commands.

content.user_title: Generate FAQ Documents from Medical Policies
content.value_statement: Turn complex medical policies into clear, patient-friendly FAQ documents. This skill helps researchers and clinicians create structured Q&A materials from dense policy protocols without manual rewriting.
actual_capabilities: Generate structured FAQ lists from medical policy and protocol documents
Adapt language complexity for different target audiences including patients, researchers, and general readers
Produce output in JSON, Markdown, or plain text formats
Validate input scope and refuse out-of-scope requests with clear error messages
Package with an executable Python script for reproducible FAQ generation runs
Separate assumptions, deliverables, risks, and unresolved items in structured output
limitations: FAQ content is templated and requires expert medical review before clinical use or publication
Does not connect to external medical databases or APIs for content enrichment or verification
Limited to English language output in the current version
Cannot interpret scanned or image-based documents without prior OCR text extraction

use_cases.title: Patient Education Material Creation
use_cases.description: A clinical researcher converts a lengthy treatment protocol into a concise FAQ for patient handouts. The skill restructures complex medical language into simple questions and answers that patients can understand.

use_cases.title: Policy Communication for Healthcare Staff
use_cases.description: A hospital administrator communicates a new infection control policy to nursing staff. The skill generates role-specific FAQs that highlight key procedural changes and responsibilities for each team.

use_cases.title: Academic Writing and Supplementary Documentation
use_cases.description: A medical writer preparing supplementary materials for a journal submission uses the skill to create a standardized FAQ appendix that addresses anticipated reviewer questions about the research methodology.

prompt_templates.title: Basic FAQ Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an FAQ from this medical protocol. Use simple language suitable for patients.
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides a single medical document and requests a straightforward FAQ

prompt_templates.title: Audience-Targeted FAQ
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an FAQ from this clinical trial protocol. Target audience: researchers. Focus on methodology, eligibility criteria, and endpoints.
prompt_templates.scenario: User specifies a target audience to get appropriately tailored language

prompt_templates.title: Formatted Output FAQ
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a Markdown-formatted FAQ from this policy document. Group questions by topic and include a summary table of contents at the top.
prompt_templates.scenario: User requests a specific output format with organizational structure

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Document FAQ with Validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these three related policy documents on patient consent. Generate a consolidated FAQ that covers all documents, flag any conflicting information between them, and list all assumptions made during synthesis.
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides multiple related documents and expects cross-referencing and validation

output_examples.input: A clinical trial protocol describing drug administration and monitoring procedures
output_examples.output: FAQ document with 8 questions covering: what the trial tests, who can participate, how the drug is administered, the monitoring schedule, potential side effects, and contact information. Each answer uses patient-friendly language without medical jargon.

output_examples.input: A hospital discharge policy with post-operative care instructions
output_examples.output: Structured FAQ organized by topic: wound care steps, medication schedule with timing, activity restrictions and duration, warning signs that require immediate attention, and follow-up appointment details. Each Q&A pair references the source section from the original policy.

best_practices: Always verify the source document is complete before starting FAQ generation since missing sections lead to incomplete coverage
Specify the target audience upfront using the audience parameter to get appropriate language complexity for patients, researchers, or general readers
Review generated FAQs against the original document to confirm medical accuracy before distributing to patients or publishing
anti_patterns: Do not use the FAQ output as a substitute for the original medical document in clinical decision-making or treatment planning
Avoid generating FAQs from documents you do not have rights or authorization to process and redistribute
Do not skip the audience parameter since generic FAQs may confuse patients with technical terms or oversimplify content intended for researchers

faq.question: What types of documents can the FAQ Generator process?
faq.answer: It processes medical policies, clinical trial protocols, treatment guidelines, and other healthcare documents provided as text input.

faq.question: Can the FAQ Generator handle non-English documents?
faq.answer: The current version is designed for English-language documents. Multi-language support is planned for a future release.

faq.question: Does the generated FAQ replace medical review?
faq.answer: No. All generated FAQs should be reviewed by a qualified medical professional before distribution to patients or publication.

faq.question: What output formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports JSON, Markdown, and plain text output formats. JSON is the default format with structured question and answer pairs.

faq.question: How does the skill handle incomplete or ambiguous source documents?
faq.answer: It reports missing information clearly, states assumptions explicitly in the output, and refuses to fabricate content when critical inputs are absent.

faq.question: Is the skill compatible with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The FAQ Generator works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through standard skill invocation in any supported environment.
